name: Julia On Slurm
summary: Run Julia scripts on Slurm with explicit depot, project, and thread settings.
description: This skill helps users run Julia batch workloads through Slurm while keeping package depots, project environments, thread counts, logs, and output paths explicit on shared HPC systems.

inputs.name: julia_script
inputs.description: Julia script to run as the batch workload.

inputs.name: julia_project
inputs.description: Julia project directory containing Project.toml for package resolution.

inputs.name: julia_depot_path
inputs.description: User-owned Julia depot path for packages, registries, artifacts, and compiled caches.

inputs.name: threads
inputs.description: Julia execution threads, usually aligned with SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK.

outputs.name: slurm_log
outputs.description: Slurm stdout/stderr log for the Julia job.

outputs.name: julia_environment_record
outputs.description: Julia version, DEPOT_PATH, LOAD_PATH, project, and thread information captured in the log.

outputs.name: analysis_output
outputs.description: User-defined Julia outputs written under the configured output directory.

examples.title: Submit a small Julia batch job
Base Julia analysis script with reproducibility output

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.
Check the Slurm wrapper shell syntax.
Replace account, partition, and module placeholders, then submit to a short test partition and confirm the log records Julia version, DEPOT_PATH, LOAD_PATH, project, and thread count.
